topic: How the plugin process is launched: the CodePathMac entrypoint
direction: S1 declares the entrypoint path in manifest; S3 produces the runnable code at that path

manifest_code_path_mac.constraint: Path relative to .sdPlugin folder root. S1 writes this into manifest.CodePathMac. S3 produces an executable shell script at exactly this path.

run_sh_contract.constraint: The shell script's effective behavior must be: cd into its own directory and exec `node main.js "$@"` so the SDK's four CLI args (-port, -pluginUUID, -registerEvent, -info) reach the Node process verbatim. The script must be marked executable (mode 0755) when packaged.

main_js_contract.constraint: Node module that, when run, parses process.argv for the four SDK CLI args, opens a WebSocket to ws://127.0.0.1:<port>, sends the registerPlugin frame, and dispatches received events to handlers.

node_runtime_assumption.rationale: Modern Node available on macOS 11+; allows native fetch/AbortController and works with bundled ws.

vendored_ws.constraint: S3 must vendor the 'ws' WebSocket library under node_modules/ relative to main.js so the plugin is self-contained and doesn't require user-side npm install.

verification: Integrator runs `./plugin/run.sh -port=12345 -pluginUUID=test -registerEvent=registerPlugin -info='{}'` against a local mock WebSocket on port 12345 and observes the registerPlugin frame arriving with the correct uuid.
